texts.full_text: of Welsh tartans. The building dates to 1820. History The Cambrian Mill is situated beside the Afon Irfon on the edge of Mynydd Epynt and the Cambrian Mountains. The Brecon Beacons and Carmarthen Fans lie to the south. The building dates to 1820, when it opened as acorn mill, which was converted to a Welsh flannel weaving mill. The mill carded, dyed, spun and wove local wool, using water power until about 1860. Before World War II (1939-45) the mill supplied woollen products to Queen Mary and Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother. It has continued to play a significant role in Welsh textile heritage even after its transition from a corn mill. Post-WWII, while many traditional mills faced closure due to competition from synthetic fibers and changing consumer preferences, Cambrian Woollen Mill managed to adapt and survived by focusing on high-quality, handcrafted products. In the mid-20th century, the mill shifted its production towards Welsh tartans, becoming renowned for its unique patterns and vibrant colors. This decision was a strategic move that capitalized on growing national pride and interest in preserving traditional crafts and cultural symbols. texts.full_text: posts, I’ve been trying to gather together my current thoughts about ‘God’. I say ‘current’, because I believe in keeping an open mind. My views, of course, are no more likely to be ‘right’ or ‘wrong’ than anyone else’s. As the apostle Paul pointed out, (though when in full dogmatic flow he seems to forget this), “Now we see a dim image in a mirror” – a ‘mirror’ because, being human, we picture God in our own image, and ‘dim’ because what is transcendental is beyond our ability to understand and describe. The existence of God, summing up my previous posts, can neither be proved nor disproved, though Richard Dawkins makes a fair point that the onus is on the ‘prover’. ‘Sacred books’ cannot ‘prove’ God’s existence, being human productions limited by the worldviews and values of the archaic cultures in which they were compiled. Since it is a collection of all kinds of books, written over many centuries, the phrase “the Bible says” is as meaningless as “the Library says”. LibIt sounds like you're reflecting on a thoughtful and nuanced approach to the topic of religion and the concept of God. Your emphasis on an open-minded and critical perspective is commendable, and the comparison to the Apostle Paul's quote is an interesting way to highlight the limitations of human understanding. The point you make about the limitations of sacred texts is also an important one, as any text is a product of the time and culture in which it was written, and therefore, carries the biases and limitations of its creators. What I found was www.morrellinternational.com The only job opportunities were positions for water bottling plant managers in the middle east, which scared me a bit, and weren't in Lonny's field, so I brushed past them, writing off this company in my head. But then, I noticed a link to "Morrell Family Charities" , which sounded interesting, so I clicked on it. That click would change our lives forever. What I found there was an amazing list of projects going on throughout the world, helping the poverty-stricken people of many countries. The link that caught my attention first was about a dry farming project in Ethiopia. I started to watch one of the videos, then pau...sed and said to Lonny, "We need to look into this more." I quickly realized that this wasn't just a charitable organization doing good work; it was a family-run charity with a hands-on approach. The projects were not merely donations but sustainable solutions aimed at empowering local communities. The video about the dry farming project in Ethiopia showed women learning how to farm without irrigation, a technique that could transform their lives and those of future generations. I turned to Lonny and said, "We need to go there. We need to see this for ourselves." He looked at me, surprised but intrigued. After some discussion and research, we decided to take the leap. We would reach out to Morrell International and express our interest in contributing to their efforts. A few weeks later, we received an invitation to visit Ethiopia. texts.full_text: during the 1940 and 1941 model years. The One-Ten designation replaced the Packard Six. Packard reintroduced a line of six-cylinder cars in 1937 after a ten-year absence as a response to the economic depression and ongoing recovery cycle in the United States. As an independent automaker, Packard could not look to other internal divisions to support its luxury models, so the inclusion of the Six, and the later 110, was necessary to aid in supporting the firm's bottom line until better times returned. Critics of the Packard Six and One-Ten have long maintained they hurt Packard's reputation of being America's premier luxury marque. Still, the reintroduction of the Six could not have come at a better time for the automaker, just prior to the nation's 1938 economic depression and the 1939-1941 economic recovery. The 1940 and 1941 Packard One-Ten, a more refined and better-equipped version of the Packard Six, helped to bolster Packard's financial stability and allowed the company to continue to focus on and support the production of its more luxurious and prestigious models. The One-Ten, while more affordable and accessible to a broader market, was still a well-equipped and well-built vehicle, and its production helped to maintain Packard's production levels and market presence. texts.full_text: ights. 450 is FL450, i.e., 45,000 feet at a standard altimeter reading. This figure is very important in flight planning. Jets typically fly around this figure for optimal aircraft/engine performance. So yes, you're right, the farther you fly from the equator, the lower you should fly. However, the following two statements are incorrect: I'd expect that the air pressure at the top of the troposphere [be the same regardless of latitude.] The pressure gradient is still the same. See: Why is the troposphere 8km higher at the equator than the poles? As the altitude of an airliner increases, the angle of attack [also increases.] As the altitude increases, so does the speed, to counter the thinner air. Until you end up in a coffin corner. Also, for high latitude polar flights, an airline can do away with the optimal performance, because the rise in temperature helps keep the fuel from freezing, however I may stand corrected on that. A: Does this mean that an airliner flying a polar route would fly at a lower altitude than one staying in the temperate or tropical regions? Yes but not "this". Your understanding of the effects of altitude is correct, except It is air density rather than air pressure that plays major role aerodynamically. Density falls with altitude similarly to pressure but a bit slower. Airplanes can compensate lower density with higher speed to avoid increasing the angle of attack, until they hit another limit. Generally, distribution of pressure and density with altitude does not depend significantly on latitude, especially if we use (as we should) the correct geopotential altitude. As

texts.full_text: perceived flaws in another person's appearance, usually a family member, caregiver, or peer. The term "by proxy" refers to the fact that the individual's body image concerns are focused onsomeone else, rather than their own appearance.This can lead to a rangeof negative emotions, including anxiety, depression, and low self-esteem. People with body dysmorphic disorder by proxy may engage inbehaviorS such as: *Excessive criticism or complaining about the perceived flaws in another person's appearance* Requesting constantreassuranceor validation from the individual they are focusedon* Avoidingsocial situationswhere the individual they arefocused onwill be present * Engaging inrumination or obsessive thinking about the perceived flaws * Feeling astrongsenseof responsibility or guilt for the perceiveD flaws The causes of body dysmorphicdisorder by proxy are not fully understood, but it is thought to be related to a combination ofgenetic, environmental, and psychological factors. It canbe a challenging condition todiagnoseand treat, as it requires a nuanced understanding ofthe complex relationships betweenindividualsand their perceptionsof each other's appearance. Treatment for body dysmorphic disorderby proxy typically involves a multidisciplinary approach, including psychotherapy, familytherapy, and education about body imageand self-esteem. It is essential to involve the individual with the condition in the treatment process, as they are often deeply invested in their own perceptions of their loved one's appearance.Early recognitionand intervention are criticalin reducing the distress and discomfortassociated with body dysmorphic disorder by proxy. texts.full_text: en manage from a single connection with ATTACH and DETACH queries. Here's an example I just ran in SQLite3's command-line client: sqlite> ATTACH 'example.sqlite' AS example; sqlite> CREATE TABLE example.ex ( a INTEGER ); sqlite> INSERT INTO example.ex VALUES (1),(2),(3); sqlite> SELECT * FROM example.ex; 1 2 3 sqlite> DETACH example; sqlite> Since the ex table is in its own file, example.sqlite, I can simply detach that DB from the connection and delete the entire file, which will be much faster. Bear in mind that the number of DBs you can attach is fairly low (with default compile options: 7). I've also read that foreign keys aren't supported in this scenario, though that info might be out of date. texts.full_text: Treatment options can vary widelydepending on several factors including age and overall health. For splenic marginal Zone B-Cell Lymphoma (SMzl), a common treatment is the removal of part, if not all parts) through an operation called 'splenectomy'. This surgery involves removing your spleens to eliminate cancer cells from that organ. In cases where complete surgical excision isn't possible or preferred due possibly for patient's comorbidities such ascardiovascular issues. Chemotherapy and radiotherapy may be considered, although these treatments are generally less effective in primary splenic lymphoma compared with other types of B-cell non-Hodgkin Lymphomas (NHL). The prognosis is typically favorable especially if the disease has not spread beyond spleen at diagnosis knownas 'localized'. With appropriate treatment and regular follow-ups, many patients can have a long-term survival. However this depends on several factors including patient's overall health status; extent of splenic involvement by lymphoma cells (how much cancer is present in your organ); how well the disease responds to initial therapy. If I try with a blank password (-p ''), it instead prints: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: FATAL: password authentication failed for user "dbuser" How do I run SchemaSpy on a PostgreSQL database with a DB user that has no password? I tried changing the pg_hba.conf settings to completely permissive, but it still doesn't work: $ sudo grep ^local /etc/postgresql/*/main/pg_hba.conf local all postgres ident local all all 0.0.0.0/0 trust local all all ::1/128 trust A: The problem is that the JDBC driver doesn't support passwordless login. It's not a SchemaSpy issue, it's a libpg-java issue. You can work around this by creating a ~/.pgpass file with the following contents: localhost:5432:dbname:dbuser: This will cause the JDBC driver to use the blank password for the connection. Note that you'll need to chmod 0600 ~/.pgpass so that only your user can read

texts.full_text: up-shift in a state where the throttle opening degree remains nearly constant (hereinafter referred to as a first up-shift mode) and an up-shift accompanying a change in the throttle opening degree, and the up-shift control operation is carried out at different rates of change in speed for each mode. Specifically, when carrying out an up-shift according to the first up-shift mode, it is performed by gradually increasing a transmission ratio with respect to time until reaching a target gear stage while keeping the throttle opening degree nearly constant; and during the up-shift accompanying a change in the throttle opening degree (second up-shift mode), when entering an accelerator operation or engine speed fluctuation such as idling fluctuation suppression control after passing through neutral zone, it is performed by quickly increasing the transmission ratio with respect to time until reaching a target gear stage while changing the throttle opening degree. However, this conventional method has room for improvement in terms of achieving better responsiveness and efficiency during up-shifts, especially when dealing with varying driving conditions or complex control strategies that may involve multiple interacting systems such as engine management, vehicle dynamics control, and advanced driver assistance systems. 3. Summary of the Invention The present invention aims to provide an improved apparatus and method for controlling a continuously variable transmission by optimizing the rate of change in speed, particularly focusing on achieving faster and smoother up-shifts while maintaining fuel efficiency and reducing drivability issues. The invention proposes using advanced control algorithms that take into account real-time vehicle dynamics, driver's intention, and system interactions to dynamically adjust the shift scheduling and acceleration profile during an up-shift process. Moreover, the present invention introduces a novel adaptive learning mechanism that continuously updates the transmission control parameters based on feedback from previous shifts, allowing for quicker adaptation to changing driving conditions and further enhancing the overall performance of the continuously variable transmission system. 4.
